extraction.one_line_summary: ALAPHILIPPE attacked the final categorized climb with 16km remaining, descended solo, and held off the chase to win by 26 seconds and take yellow.
tactical_patterns: WELLENS attacked breakaway companions 5km before summit to secure KOM jersey even when caught for stage, prioritizing classification points.
ALAPHILIPPE's solo attack on final categorized climb succeeded because he gained time on descent, not climb, exploiting handling skills.
VAN AERT finished second in both road sprint and GC, revealing Grand Tour stage racing capability beyond cyclocross/time trial specialization.

rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: Won by attacking 16km out and descending solo, showing exceptional descending skills and power management for sustained solo effort.
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: Second overall and best young rider in first-ever Grand Tour, demonstrating unexpected stage racing capacity beyond known TT/cyclocross skills.
rider_signals.WELLENS Tim: positive: Attacked breakaway specifically to secure KOM jersey via time bonuses even when caught, showing tactical classification awareness.
rider_signals.MARTIN Tony: neutral: Paced breakaway for 1.5 hours as domestique work before falling away, fulfilling team role rather than personal ambition.
